requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier onboarding process undergoes internal approval workflow.
Approved supplier receives invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Supplier certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, and deactivation/reactivation.
Approval workflows involve Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams.
Coupa SIM integrates with SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or specific supplier types, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update vendor information in Coupa, including status changes.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Coupa displays supplier record status and Coupa Supplier Portal status for easy tracking.
The supplier onboarding and supplier management process will be standardized across geographies, with exceptions for Australia in approval workflows and local guidelines for information requirements.
Coupa supports auto-translation of standard information and requires custom information translation for specific geographies.
Process owners and document reviewers can acknowledge and sign the document.
